heading: Firefighter assistance
label: 2229 Firefighter assistance

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of all functions, personnel, assets, components, authorities, grant programs, and liabilities of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, including the functions of the Under Secretary for Federal Emergency Management relating thereto, to the Federal Emergency Management Agency, see , Domestic Security.section 315(a)(1) of Title 6For transfer of functions, personnel, assets, and liabilities of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, including the functions of the Director of the Federal Emergency Management Agency relating thereto, to the Secretary of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see former section 313(1) and sections 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6

Training on Administration of Fire Grant Programs, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–98, § 5131 Stat. 2240Jan. 3, 2018“(a).—In GeneralThe Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, acting through the Administrator of the United States Fire Administration, may develop and make widely available an electronic, online training course for members of the fire and emergency response community on matters relating to the administration of grants under sections 33 and 34 of the Federal Fire Prevention and Control Act of 1974 ( and 2229a).15 U.S.C. 2229“(b).—RequirementsThe Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency shall ensure that any training developed and made available under subsection (a) is—“(1)tailored to the financial and time constraints of members of the fire and emergency response community; and“(2)accessible to all individuals in the career, combination, paid-on-call, and volunteer fire and emergency response community.”

Framework for Oversight and Monitoring of the Assistance to Firefighters Grants Program, the Fire Prevention and Safety Grants Program, and the Staffing for Adequate Fire and Emergency Response Grant Program, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–98, § 6131 Stat. 2241Jan. 3, 2018“(a).—FrameworkNot later than 90 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency, acting through the Administrator of the United States Fire Administration, shall develop and implement a grant monitoring and oversight framework to mitigate and minimize risks of fraud, waste, abuse, and mismanagement relating to the grants programs under sections 33 and 34 of the Federal Fire Prevention and Control Act of 1974 ( and 2229a).Jan. 3, 201815 U.S.C. 2229“(b).—ElementsThe framework required under subsection (a) shall include the following:“(1)Developing standardized guidance and training for all participants in the grant programs described in subsection (a).“(2)Conducting regular risk assessments.“(3)Conducting desk reviews and site visits.“(4)Enforcement actions to recoup potential questionable costs of grant recipients.“(5)Such other oversight and monitoring tools as the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency considers necessary to mitigate and minimize fraud, waste, abuse, and mismanagement relating to the grant programs described in subsection (a).”

children.heading: Administrator of FEMA
children.label: 1 Administrator of FEMA
children.text: The term “Administrator of FEMA” means the Administrator of FEMA, acting through the Administrator.

children.heading: Available grant funds
children.label: 2 Available grant funds
children.text: The term “available grant funds”, with respect to a fiscal year, means those funds appropriated pursuant to the authorization of appropriations in subsection (q)(1) for such fiscal year less any funds used for administrative costs pursuant to subsection (q)(2) in such fiscal year.

children.heading: Career fire department
children.label: 3 Career fire department
children.text: The term “career fire department” means a fire department that has an all-paid force of firefighting personnel other than paid-on-call firefighters.

children.label: A
children.text: paid firefighting personnel; and

children.label: B
children.text: volunteer firefighting personnel.

children.heading: Firefighting personnel
children.label: 5 Firefighting personnel
children.text: The term “firefighting personnel” means individuals, including volunteers, who are firefighters, officers of fire departments, or emergency medical service personnel of fire departments.

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 6 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given such term in .section 1001 of title 20

children.heading: Nonaffiliated EMS organization
children.label: 7 Nonaffiliated EMS organization
children.text: The term “nonaffiliated EMS organization” means a public or private nonprofit emergency medical services organization that is not affiliated with a hospital and does not serve a geographic area in which the Administrator of FEMA finds that emergency medical services are adequately provided by a fire department.

children.heading: Paid-on-call
children.label: 8 Paid-on-call
children.text: The term “paid-on-call” with respect to firefighting personnel means firefighting personnel who are paid a stipend for each event to which they respond.

children.heading: Volunteer fire department
children.label: 9 Volunteer fire department
children.text: The term “volunteer fire department” means a fire department that has an all-volunteer force of firefighting personnel.

children.label: A
children.text: assistance to firefighters grants under subsection (c); and

children.label: B
children.text: fire prevention and safety grants and other assistance under subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: establish specific criteria for the selection of grant recipients under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide assistance with application preparation to applicants for such grants.

children.label: A
children.text: fire departments, for the purpose of protecting the health and safety of the public and firefighting personnel throughout the United States against fire, fire-related, and other hazards;

children.label: B
children.text: nonaffiliated EMS organizations to support the provision of emergency medical services; and

children.label: C
children.text: State fire training academies for the purposes described in subparagraphs (G), (H), and (I) of paragraph (3).

children.label: i
children.text: In the case of a recipient that serves a jurisdiction with 100,000 people or fewer, the amount of the grant awarded to such recipient shall not exceed $1,000,000 in any fiscal year.

children.label: ii
children.text: In the case of a recipient that serves a jurisdiction with more than 100,000 people but not more than 500,000 people, the amount of the grant awarded to such recipient shall not exceed $2,000,000 in any fiscal year.

children.label: iii
children.text: In the case of a recipient that serves a jurisdiction with more than 500,000 but not more than 1,000,000 people, the amount of the grant awarded to such recipient shall not exceed $3,000,000 in any fiscal year.

children.label: iv
children.text: In the case of a recipient that serves a jurisdiction with more than 1,000,000 people but not more than 2,500,000 people, the amount of the grant awarded to such recipient shall not exceed $6,000,000 for any fiscal year.

children.label: v
children.text: In the case of a recipient that serves a jurisdiction with more than 2,500,000 people, the amount of the grant awarded to such recipient shall not exceed $9,000,000 in any fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraphs (A) and (B) and except as provided under clause (ii), the Administrator of FEMA may not award a grant under this subsection in a fiscal year in an amount that exceeds the amount that is one percent of the available grant funds in such fiscal year.1So in original.1

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: The Administrator of FEMA may waive the limitation in clause (i) with respect to a grant recipient if the Administrator of FEMA determines that such recipient has an extraordinary need for a grant in an amount that exceeds the limit under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: firefighting;

children.label: ii
children.text: emergency medical services and other emergency response (including response to natural disasters, acts of terrorism, and other man-made disasters);

children.label: iii
children.text: arson prevention and detection;

children.label: iv
children.text: maritime firefighting; or

children.label: v
children.text: the handling of hazardous materials.

children.label: B
children.text: To train firefighting personnel to provide any of the training described under subparagraph (A).

children.label: C
children.text: To fund the creation of rapid intervention teams to protect firefighting personnel at the scenes of fires and other emergencies.

children.label: i
children.text: fire inspectors; and

children.label: I
children.text: whose responsibilities include fire safety inspections; and

children.label: II
children.text: who are employed by or serving as volunteers with a fire department.

children.label: E
children.text: To establish wellness and fitness programs for firefighting personnel to ensure that the firefighting personnel are able to carry out their duties as firefighters, including programs dedicated to raising awareness of, and prevention of, job-related mental health issues.

children.label: F
children.text: To fund emergency medical services provided by fire departments and nonaffiliated EMS organizations.

children.label: G
children.text: To acquire additional firefighting vehicles, including fire trucks and other apparatus.

children.label: i
children.text: fighting fires with foam in remote areas without access to water; and

children.label: ii
children.text: communications, monitoring, and response to a natural disaster, act of terrorism, or other man-made disaster, including the use of a weapon of mass destruction.

children.label: i
children.text: prescribed for firefighting personnel by the Occupational Safety and Health Administration of the Department of Labor; or

children.label: ii
children.text: for responding to a natural disaster or act of terrorism or other man-made disaster, including the use of a weapon of mass destruction.

children.label: J
children.text: To modify fire stations, fire training facilities, and other facilities to protect the health and safety of firefighting personnel.

children.label: K
children.text: To educate the public about arson prevention and detection.

children.label: L
children.text: To provide incentives for the recruitment and retention of volunteer firefighting personnel for volunteer firefighting departments and other firefighting departments that utilize volunteers.

children.label: M
children.text: To support such other activities, consistent with the purposes of this subsection, as the Administrator of FEMA determines appropriate.

children.label: N
children.text: To provide specialized training to firefighters, paramedics, emergency medical service workers, and other first responders to recognize individuals who have mental illness and how to properly intervene with individuals with mental illness, including strategies for verbal de-escalation of crisis.

children.heading: Fire prevention and safety grants
children.label: d Fire prevention and safety grants
A
children.text: award grants to fire departments;

children.label: i
children.text: fire prevention programs; and

children.label: ii
children.text: research to improve firefighter health and life safety; and

children.label: C
children.text: award grants to institutions of higher education, national fire service organizations, or national fire safety organizations to establish and operate fire safety research centers.

children.heading: Maximum grant amount
children.label: 2 Maximum grant amount
children.text: A grant awarded under this subsection may not exceed $1,500,000 for a fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: To enforce fire codes and promote compliance with fire safety standards.

children.label: B
children.text: To fund fire prevention programs, including programs that educate the public about arson prevention and detection.

children.label: C
children.text: To fund wildland fire prevention programs, including education, awareness, and mitigation programs that protect lives, property, and natural resources from fire in the wildland-urban interface.

children.label: D
children.text: In the case of a grant awarded under paragraph (1)(C), to fund the establishment or operation of a fire safety research center for the purpose of significantly reducing the number of fire-related deaths and injuries among firefighters and the general public through research, development, and technology transfer activities.

children.label: E
children.text: To support such other activities, consistent with the purposes of this subsection, as the Administrator of FEMA determines appropriate.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 4 Limitation
children.text: None of the funds made available under this subsection may be provided to the Association of Community Organizations for Reform Now (ACORN) or any of its affiliates, subsidiaries, or allied organizations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An entity seeking a grant under this section shall submit to the Administrator of FEMA an application therefor in such form and in such manner as the Administrator of FEMA determines appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: A description of the financial need of the applicant for the grant.

children.label: B
children.text: An analysis of the costs and benefits, with respect to public safety, of the use for which a grant is requested.

children.label: C
children.text: An agreement to provide information to the national fire incident reporting system for the period covered by the grant.

children.label: i
children.text: for the same purpose for which the application for a grant under this section was submitted; or

children.label: ii
children.text: from the Federal Government for other fire-related purposes.

children.label: E
children.text: Such other information as the Administrator of FEMA determines appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Two or more entities may submit an application under paragraph (1) for a grant under this section to fund a joint program or initiative, including acquisition of shared equipment or vehicles.

children.heading: Nonexclusivity
children.label: B Nonexclusivity
children.text: Applications under this paragraph may be submitted instead of or in addition to any other application submitted under paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: publish guidance on applying for and administering grants awarded for joint programs and initiatives described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: encourage applicants to apply for grants for joint programs and initiatives described in subparagraph (A) as the Administrator of FEMA determines appropriate to achieve greater cost effectiveness and regional efficiency.

children.heading: Peer review of grant applications
children.label: f Peer review of grant applications

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator of FEMA shall, after consultation with national fire service and emergency medical services organizations, appoint fire service personnel to conduct peer reviews of applications received under subsection (e)(1).

children.heading: Applicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: 2 Applicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply to activities carried out pursuant to this subsection.
The findings and recommendations of the peer reviews carried out under subsection (f).
The degree to which an award will reduce deaths, injuries, and property damage by reducing the risks associated with fire-related and other hazards.
The extent of the need of an applicant for a grant under this section and the need to protect the United States as a whole.
The number of calls requesting or requiring a fire fighting or emergency medical response received by an applicant.
not less than 25 percent are awarded under subsection (c) to career fire departments;
not less than 25 percent are awarded under subsection (c) to volunteer fire departments;
not less than 25 percent are awarded under subsection (c) to combination fire departments and fire departments using paid-on-call firefighting personnel;

not less than 10 percent are available for open competition among career fire departments, volunteer fire departments, combination fire departments, and fire departments using paid-on-call firefighting personnel for grants awarded under subsection (c);
not less than 10 percent are awarded under subsection (d); and
not more than 2 percent are awarded under this section to nonaffiliated EMS organizations described in subsection (c)(1)(B).

children.heading: Funding for emergency medical services
children.label: 1 Funding for emergency medical services
children.text: Not less than 3.5 percent of the available grant funds for a fiscal year shall be awarded under this section for purposes described in subsection (c)(3)(F).

children.heading: Maximum share
children.label: A Maximum share
children.text: Not more than 3 percent of the available grant funds for a fiscal year may be awarded under subsection (c)(1)(C).

children.heading: Maximum grant amount
children.label: B Maximum grant amount
children.text: The Administrator of FEMA may not award a grant under subsection (c)(1)(C) to a State fire training academy in an amount that exceeds $1,000,000 in any fiscal year.

children.heading: Amounts for purchasing firefighting vehicles
children.label: 3 Amounts for purchasing firefighting vehicles
children.text: Not more than 25 percent of the available grant funds for a fiscal year may be used to assist grant recipients to purchase vehicles pursuant to subsection (c)(3)(G).

children.heading: Assistance to firefighters grants to fire departments
children.label: 1 Assistance to firefighters grants to fire departments
A
children.text: the extent to which the grant would enhance the daily operations of the applicant and the impact of such a grant on the protection of lives and property; and

children.label: i
children.text: Population served.

children.label: ii
children.text: Geographic response area.

children.label: iii
children.text: Hazards vulnerability.

children.label: iv
children.text: Call volume.

children.label: v
children.text: Financial situation, including unemployment rate of the area being served.

children.label: vi
children.text: Need for training or equipment.

children.heading: Applications from nonaffiliated EMS organizations
children.label: 2 Applications from nonaffiliated EMS organizations
children.text: In the case of an application submitted under subsection (e)(1) by a nonaffiliated EMS organization, the Administrator of FEMA shall consider the extent to which other sources of Federal funding are available to the applicant to provide the assistance requested in such application.

children.heading: Awarding fire prevention and safety grants to certain organizations that are not fire departments
children.label: 3 Awarding fire prevention and safety grants to certain organizations that are not fire departments
A
children.text: prevention of injuries to high risk groups from fire; and

children.label: B
children.text: research programs that demonstrate a potential to improve firefighter safety.

children.heading: Awarding grants for fire safety research centers
children.label: 4 Awarding grants for fire safety research centers
I
children.text: the demonstrated research and extension resources available to the recipient to carry out the research, development, and technology transfer activities;

children.label: II
children.text: the capability of the recipient to provide leadership in making national contributions to fire safety;

children.label: III
children.text: the recipient’s ability to disseminate the results of fire safety research; and

children.label: IV
children.text: the strategic plan the recipient proposes to carry out under the grant;

children.label: I
children.text: a national fire service organization or a national fire safety organization; and

children.label: II
children.text: an institution of higher education, including a minority-serving institution (as described in ); andsection 1067q(a) of title 20

children.label: iii
children.text: consider the research needs identified and prioritized through the workshop required by subparagraph (B)(i).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Administrator of FEMA shall convene a workshop of the fire safety research community, fire service organizations, and other appropriate stakeholders to identify and prioritize fire safety research needs.January 2, 2013

children.heading: Publication
children.label: ii Publication
children.text: The Administrator of FEMA shall ensure that the results of the workshop are made available to the public.

children.heading: Limitations on grants for fire safety research centers
children.label: C Limitations on grants for fire safety research centers

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Administrator of FEMA may award grants under subsection (d) to establish not more than 3 fire safety research centers.

children.heading: Recipients
children.label: ii Recipients
children.text: An institution of higher education, a national fire service organization, and a national fire safety organization may not directly receive a grant under subsection (d) for a fiscal year for more than 1 fire safety research center.

children.heading: Avoiding duplication
children.label: 5 Avoiding duplication
children.text: The Administrator of FEMA shall review lists submitted by applicants pursuant to subsection (e)(2)(D) and take such actions as the Administrator of FEMA considers necessary to prevent unnecessary duplication of grant awards.

children.heading: Matching and maintenance of expenditure requirements
children.label: k Matching and maintenance of expenditure requirements

children.heading: Matching requirement for assistance to firefighters grants
children.label: 1 Matching requirement for assistance to firefighters grants

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), an applicant seeking a grant to carry out an activity under subsection (c) shall agree to make available non-Federal funds to carry out such activity in an amount equal to not less than 15 percent of the grant awarded to such applicant under such subsection.

children.heading: Exception for entities serving small communities
children.label: B Exception for entities serving small communities
i
children.text: more than 20,000 residents but not more than 1,000,000 residents, the application shall agree to make available non-Federal funds in an amount equal to not less than 10 percent of the grant awarded to such applicant under such subsection; and3So in original. Probably should be “applicant”.3

children.label: ii
children.text: 20,000 residents or fewer, the applicant shall agree to make available non-Federal funds in an amount equal to not less than 5 percent of the grant awarded to such applicant under such subsection.

children.heading: Matching requirement for fire prevention and safety grants
children.label: 2 Matching requirement for fire prevention and safety grants

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An applicant seeking a grant to carry out an activity under subsection (d) shall agree to make available non-Federal funds to carry out such activity in an amount equal to not less than 5 percent of the grant awarded to such applicant under such subsection.

children.heading: Means of matching
children.label: B Means of matching
children.text: An applicant for a grant under subsection (d) may meet the matching requirement under subparagraph (A) through direct funding, funding of complementary activities, or the provision of staff, facilities, services, material, or equipment.

children.heading: Maintenance of expenditures
children.label: 3 Maintenance of expenditures
children.text: An applicant seeking a grant under subsection (c) or (d) shall agree to maintain during the term of the grant the applicant’s aggregate expenditures relating to the uses described in subsections (c)(3) and (d)(3) at not less than 80 percent of the average amount of such expenditures in the 2 fiscal years preceding the fiscal year in which the grant amounts are received.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C)(ii), the Administrator of FEMA may waive or reduce the requirements of paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) in cases of demonstrated economic hardship.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Administrator of FEMA shall establish and publish guidelines for determining what constitutes economic hardship for purposes of this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: recognized for expertise in firefighting, emergency medical services provided by fire services, or the economic affairs of State and local governments; and

children.label: II
children.text: members of national fire service organizations or national organizations representing the interests of State and local governments.

children.label: I
children.text: Changes in rates of unemployment from previous years.

children.label: II
children.text: Whether the rates of unemployment of the relevant communities are currently and have consistently exceeded the annual national average rates of unemployment.4So in original. Probably should be “are currently exceeding and have consistently exceeded”.4

children.label: III
children.text: Changes in percentages of individuals eligible to receive food stamps from previous years.

children.label: IV
children.text: Such other factors as the Administrator of FEMA considers appropriate.

children.heading: Certain applicants for fire prevention and safety grants
children.label: C Certain applicants for fire prevention and safety grants
i
children.text: is described in subsection (d)(1)(B); and

children.label: ii
children.text: is not a fire department or emergency medical services organization.

children.label: i
children.text: the process for applying for grants under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the criteria that will be used for selecting grant recipients; and

children.label: B
children.text: an explanation of any differences between such guidelines and the recommendations obtained under paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: Criteria for the awarding of grants under this section.

children.label: ii
children.text: Administrative changes to the assistance program established under subsection (b).

children.label: i
children.text: is recognized for expertise in firefighting or emergency medical services;

children.label: ii
children.text: is not an employee of the Federal Government; and

children.label: I
children.text: providers of emergency medical services that are affiliated with fire departments; or

children.label: II
children.text: nonaffiliated EMS providers.

children.heading: Applicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: 3 Applicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply to activities carried out under this subsection.

children.heading: Accounting determination
children.label: m Accounting determination
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, for purposes of this section, equipment costs shall include all costs attributable to any design, purchase of components, assembly, manufacture, and transportation of equipment not otherwise commercially available.

children.heading: Eligible grantee on behalf of Alaska Native villages
children.label: n Eligible grantee on behalf of Alaska Native villages
children.text: The Alaska Village Initiatives, a non-profit organization incorporated in the State of Alaska, shall be eligible to apply for and receive a grant or other assistance under this section on behalf of Alaska Native villages.

children.heading: Training standards
children.label: o Training standards
children.text: If an applicant for a grant under this section is applying for such grant to purchase training that does not meet or exceed any applicable national voluntary consensus standards, including those developed under , the applicant shall submit to the Administrator of FEMA an explanation of the reasons that the training proposed to be purchased will serve the needs of the applicant better than training that meets or exceeds such standards.section 747 of title 6

children.heading: Ensuring effective use of grants
children.label: p Ensuring effective use of grants
A
children.text: the grant amounts are expended for the intended purposes; and

children.label: B
children.text: the grant recipient complies with the requirements of subsection (k).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administrator of FEMA shall develop and implement a performance assessment system, including quantifiable performance metrics, to evaluate the extent to which grants awarded under this section are furthering the purposes of this section, including protecting the health and safety of the public and firefighting personnel against fire and fire-related hazards.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: B Consultation
children.text: The Administrator of FEMA shall consult with fire service representatives and with the Comptroller General of the United States in developing the assessment system required by subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Annual reports to Administrator of FEMA
children.label: 3 Annual reports to Administrator of FEMA
children.text: Not less frequently than once each year during the term of a grant awarded under this section, the recipient of the grant shall submit to the Administrator of FEMA an annual report describing how the recipient used the grant amounts.

children.label: i
children.text: information on the performance assessment system developed under paragraph (2); and

children.label: ii
children.text: using the performance metrics developed under such paragraph, an evaluation of the effectiveness of the grants awarded under this section.

children.heading: Additional information
children.label: B Additional information
children.text: The report due under subparagraph (A) on , shall also include recommendations for legislative changes to improve grants under this section.September 30, 2016

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $750,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2024 through 2028.

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: 2 Administrative expenses
children.text: Of the amounts appropriated pursuant to paragraph (1) for a fiscal year, the Administrator of FEMA may use not more than 5 percent of such amounts for salaries and expenses and other administrative costs incurred by the Administrator of FEMA in the course of awarding grants and providing assistance under this section.

children.heading: Congressionally directed spending
children.label: 3 Congressionally directed spending
children.text: Consistent with the requirements in subsections (c)(1) and (d)(1) that grants under those subsections be awarded on a competitive basis, none of the funds appropriated pursuant to this subsection may be used for any congressionally directed spending item (as defined under the rules of the Senate and the House of Representatives).

children.heading: Sunset of authorities
children.label: r Sunset of authorities
children.text: The authority to award assistance and grants under this section shall expire on .September 30, 2030
